page: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the E2 instance family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz. The configuration provides 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations place the e2-standard-2 in the lower performance tiers. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance scored 1103.39, while multi-core performance reached 1451.58. Memory bandwidth tests show 79.92 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 11.77 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Geekbench results reflect similar constraints, with a single-core score of 867.0 and a multi-core score of 1025.0. Cryptographic and compression tasks also align with these lower-tier metrics, as seen in the Passmark CPU encryption score of 2113.08 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 18.58 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the e2-standard-2 offers a highly accessible entry point for workloads that do not demand high-performance computing or high-speed memory access. The resource density is balanced for lightweight applications. It is best suited for low-traffic web serving, basic database operations, lightweight background tasks, development environments, and small-scale microservices where resource utilization remains predictable and modest.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-2 is an entry-level, general-purpose instance configured with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz, this virtual machine utilizes 1 physical core with hyper-threading. It features no local storage and no integrated GPU resources. Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance is positioned in the lower tiers, with a Geekbench single-core score of 867.0 and a multi-core score of 1025.0. Memory bandwidth is constrained, measuring 11.77 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Given its hardware profile, the e2-standard-2 is qualitatively cost-efficient for low-demand applications. It is best suited for lightweight workloads such as static web serving, development environments, small-scale database operations, and basic background processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-2 is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It is designed for cost-efficient, low-demand workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-2 is a general-purpose VM with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, suitable for lightweight workloads and development.
tagline: A cost-efficient, general-purpose virtual machine with balanced memory and compute resources for lightweight, entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory on an x86_64 architecture
Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators included
Fitted for low-traffic static web serving and basic database tasks
Cost-efficient option for development and testing environments
